steps.title: Track the shared mood
steps.content: The three underlined phrases compare Durant to a culprit, someone surrendering to justice, and someone expecting a rebuke. Those images all point to guilt, fear, and uneasiness. The surrounding sentence says this happens during his ordinary morning trip to work, even though the text gives no actual wrongdoing or specific mistake.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported explanations
steps.content:
• Choice A - They illustrate an unexplained nervous tension that Durant experiences as he begins each workday.: this captures the repeated guilty and anxious imagery without inventing a specific cause.
  • Choice B - They suggest Durant's dismay about being trapped in a job that has become exceedingly monotonous.: the routine is monotonous, but the underlined phrases stress fear, not boredom.
  • Choice C - They reveal that Durant is unwaveringly precise in his daily routine because of the unreasonable expectations of his employers.: the text does not blame employer expectations.
  • Choice D - They convey Durant's growing apprehensiveness about having failed to complete an important work task for the first time in his long career.: no first-time failure is stated.
The answer is A.
